findings.evidence: Line 34 applies `@[expose]` to the whole public section; lines 131-149 and 234-252 already provide simp-normal forms for the two quotient equivalences, so exposing their first-isomorphism-theorem construction is unnecessary public surface.
findings.fix: Remove the section-level `@[expose]`; keep the declarations public but opaque, and add `@[expose]` only to a specific definition if a downstream target demonstrably must compute by unfolding it.
findings.issue: `@[expose] public section` exposes the bodies of the quotient equivalences, homs, kernels, and helper lemmas even though consumers can use the public `*_mk`, `coe_*_mk`, `*_eq_zero_iff`, and membership lemmas without unfolding them.

summary: The quotient API is characterized by the expected `mk` and coercion simp lemmas, but the file exposes every new body instead of keeping the implementation opaque.
